user.name: David P
user.bio: Meditation, music, trading

comment.body:
think like this:
delta = premium to spot
vanna = (premium to spot) to vol = delta to vol

gamma = (premium to spot) to spot = delta to spot
zomma = [(premium to spot) to spot] to vol = (delta to spot) to vol = gamma to vol 

but it can also be written as
[(premium to spot) to vol] to spot = (delta to vol) to spot = vanna to spot 

...assuming the option pricing function is smooth

comment.body:
1. Likely long ATM call to get maximum gamma to neutralise their negative delta exposure for that 2 hour window.

2. For upside: We should see vol decline , vix below 20.7 , mms would buy futures as delta compresses. 
We should see call ivs rise or stabilise 

For downside : vol spike, vix rise above 20.70 , otm puts get bid , 

3.  Prove: Flattening put skew, 5d put iv falling, Also Pinning near the 5600 strike, ( as mms are buying futures near here to neutralise their negative delta) 

Disprove: longer put skew, break below 5600 , put open interest growing significantly  5550 and belwo

comment.body:
1) It could be that it depends on whether they are above or below the put’s strike, during the last two hours, which essentially acts as 0 Vanna . If they are above, they might opt for a long OTM call since it has long Vanna and the market makers become shorter Vega, thus increasing volatility. In other words, they choose an OTM call that has long Vanna (higher prob it can go ITM take advantage of the upside move, as stated in the brochure “investors may forgo some upside”). If they are below, they do the opposite: they would sell OTM calls because they are short Vanna and the market makers become longer Vega, thereby decreasing volatility.

Both calls would hedge the delta but with different effects based on Vanna. (I thought like this, hope it makes sense)

comment.body:
2) in primis we should see at vol because this is the variable that can shift the mechanism, we want vol to declines in order to stabilise the spot around 5767 for bulls
For bears we will look at the put if can catch a bid in order to see downside toward 5521 support.
As said before vol in decisive as we know that above 5667 speed turns flat to positive, that creates a net longer gamma environment that favours stabilisation. We can see it from that hidden bullish behaviour that rate of changes for 22d put and 22d call relative to ATM is telling us. In fact even if IV is steadily declined we see put to call skew longer. This still favours for stabilization. In addition we want to see day by day the mkt above youre coded pivot to confirm the bullish thesis. Last but not least we need to pay attention at vix tails that gives stability in the short term but, call IV has increased compared to one month ago and IV put declined (shorter skew overall) this could be our warning sign so better to keep our eyes on that.

comment.body:
Conclusion
The data inputs provide mean reversion thresholds for SPX trading, likely based on a mean of 5667.56 and standard deviation of ~32, with levels at 1 SD (68.2%), 2 SD (95.4%), and 3 SD (99.73%). Use upper levels to identify sell opportunities, lower levels for buys, and Risk levels for stops. Leverage the 5-minute, 10-minute, and 15-minute timeframes to confirm signals, enhancing trade reliability by seeking confluence across intervals. This strategy capitalizes on price extremes, expecting reversion to the mean, tailored to your risk and trading style.
